bp.$description:
Breakpoints — SHARED VALUES READ BY JS, not media queries.
Components style themselves with inline style objects, which cannot hold a
media query, so responsive branches are JS (see use-container-width.js).
These live here anyway so the thresholds stay a token, not a magic number
retyped in four components. 768px is what DAMA hardcodes.

dz.$description:
Density (H7 rev.3) — comfortable by default. Data-dense surfaces read these
instead of hard-coded px so a single scope switch re-densifies rows and controls.

text.$description: control text — buttons, inputs, selects, menu items, table cells

text-md.$description: secondary control text — tag chips, pagination, secondary buttons

text-sm.$description: secondary control text — hints, validation errors, badges, legends

text-xs.$description: micro control text — field labels, shortcuts, eyebrow labels

lh.$description: glyph-tight line height — the control reset. Unlike every other dz member this is unitless, so it carries its own $type override: a line height is a number, not a dimension, the same distinction that puts lh in typography.json to begin with. Used wherever a box must be exactly its glyph — an icon inside a button, a standalone icon, an icon-only close/remove control — so the line box never throws the surrounding control out of alignment.
